title: hieracium murorum subsp. viridicollum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. viridicollum, also known as the wall hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of wall hawkweed include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma. Wall hawkweed can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in conjunction with other medicinal plants to enhance its therapeutic effects.
